heading: Admissibility of documentation maintained in foreign countries
label: 982 Admissibility of documentation maintained in foreign countries
notes: Amendments1984—Subsec. (d)(3), (4). redesignated par. (4) as (3) and struck out former par. (3) which provided that an item was to be treated as foreign connected if directly or indirectly from a source outside the United States, or the item (in whole or in part) purported to arise outside the United States, or was otherwise dependent on transactions occurring outside the United States.Pub. L. 98–369

L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Sept. 3, 1982Oct. 22, 1986“The amendments made by this section [enacting this section] shall apply with respect to formal document requests (as defined in section 982(c)(1) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954], as added by this section) mailed after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”Sept. 3, 1982

children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: If the taxpayer fails to substantially comply with any formal document request arising out of the examination of the tax treatment of any item (hereinafter in this section referred to as the “examined item”) before the 90th day after the date of the mailing of such request on motion by the Secretary, any court having jurisdiction of a civil proceeding in which the tax treatment of the examined item is an issue shall prohibit the introduction by the taxpayer of any foreign-based documentation covered by such request.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subsection (a) shall not apply with respect to any documentation if the taxpayer establishes that the failure to provide the documentation as requested by the Secretary is due to reasonable cause.

children.heading: Foreign nondisclosure law not reasonable cause
children.label: 2 Foreign nondisclosure law not reasonable cause
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), the fact that a foreign jurisdiction would impose a civil or criminal penalty on the taxpayer (or any other person) for disclosing the requested documentation is not reasonable cause.

children.label: A
children.text: the time and place for the production of the documentation,

children.label: B
children.text: a statement of the reason the documentation previously produced (if any) is not sufficient,

children.label: C
children.text: a description of the documentation being sought, and

children.label: D
children.text: the consequences to the taxpayer of the failure to produce the documentation described in subparagraph (C).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other law or rule of law, any person to whom a formal document request is mailed shall have the right to begin a proceeding to quash such request not later than the 90th day after the day such request was mailed. In any such proceeding, the Secretary may seek to compel compliance with such request.

children.heading: Jurisdiction
children.label: B Jurisdiction
children.text: The United States district court for the district in which the person (to whom the formal document request is mailed) resides or is found shall have jurisdiction to hear any proceeding brought under subparagraph (A). An order denying the petition shall be deemed a final order which may be appealed.

children.heading: Suspension of 90-day period
children.label: C Suspension of 90-day period
children.text: The running of the 90-day period referred to in subsection (a) shall be suspended during any period during which a proceeding brought under subparagraph (A) is pending.

children.heading: Foreign-based documentation
children.label: 1 Foreign-based documentation
children.text: The term “foreign-based documentation” means any documentation which is outside the United States and which may be relevant or material to the tax treatment of the examined item.

children.heading: Documentation
children.label: 2 Documentation
children.text: The term “documentation” includes books and records.

children.heading: Authority to extend 90-day period
children.label: 3 Authority to extend 90-day period
children.text: The Secretary, and any court having jurisdiction over a proceeding under subsection (c)(2), may extend the 90-day period referred to in subsection (a).

children.heading: Suspension of statute of limitations
children.label: e Suspension of statute of limitations
children.text: If any person takes any action as provided in subsection (c)(2), the running of any period of limitations under section 6501 (relating to the assessment and collection of tax) or under section 6531 (relating to criminal prosecutions) with respect to such person shall be suspended for the period during which the proceeding under such subsection, and appeals therein, are pending.
